hypothesis: If a Δ-learning XGBoost model trained to predict the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT band gaps using the ST-135, RAC, and APRDF descriptor sets (top 400 features) is applied to 4941 MOFs, then it will yield lower prediction error (for both PBE and HSE06 references) than a direct XGBoost model predicting DFT band gaps, because modeling the systematic offset from a cheap baseline reduces the target error.
